Kimi

AI

Artificial Intelligence

AI Agent

Machine Learning

Moonshot AI just shipped a 2.8 trillion parameter model that beats Claude Fable 5 in the lanes it cares about, tripled its own prices, and called it open. Two of those three things are true right now.

11 min read
Just now

--

On July 16 Moonshot AI released Kimi K3 and the news was huge: 2.8 trillion parameters, the open-weight model anyone has ever released. This is a big deal and the news stories treated it like a big announcement. VentureBeat said it is the open-source model ever. Fortune said it makes Chinese AI as good as the best. Bloomberg said it is closing the gap. All of that is true.
But there is something that most of the news stories did not mention and it changes how we should think about the whole release: the weights are not available yet. Moonshot AI says they will be available by July 27. Until then K3 is a model that you can only use through a website, like the other models it is being compared to and the word “open” in all those news stories is something that will happen later not something you can use now.
I want to think about K3 in a way because it deserves it. This is not a model trying to be something it is not. It is an attempt to be the best from a…
